_note: Liu Bei rescues Kong Rong at Beihai (Yellow Turban remnants); Cao Cao's Xuzhou campaign continues; Lu Bu raids Yanzhou. All 194.
Cao Cao retakes Yanzhou from Lu Bu — text explicitly says '(AD 195)'. Tao Qian dies and Liu Bei accepts Xuzhou.
Li Jue and Guo Si feud in Changan; Emperor flees east; Anyi declared temporary capital. All 195.
Cao Cao moves the court to Xuchang — text explicitly says '(AD 196)' for the reign-style change to Jian'an. Lu Bu's night raid on Xuzhou.
Sun Ce's southern conquest: defeats Liu Yao, Taishi Ci, and White Tiger Yan to take Jiangdong. Campaign ran late 195 through 196.
Lu Bu's halberd shot at the camp gate (196); Yuan Shu attacks Liu Bei; Cao Cao's catastrophic defeat at River Yu vs Zhang Xiu (early 197, death of Dian Wei + Cao Ang).
Yuan Shu declares himself emperor of Zhong (Cheng); seven armies march on Xuzhou; the alliance forms against him. Spring–summer 197.
Jia Xu's countermove at Wancheng (late 197); Xiahou Dun loses his eye to Cao Xing during the Xuzhou siege of Liu Bei (198).
Cao Cao floods Xiapi (winter 198); Lu Bu strangled at White Gate Tower (Feb 199 per Sanguozhi). Zhang Liao taken in.
Hunt at Xutian; Dong Cheng's secret-decree conspiracy formed (199 winter / 200 spring) — leads directly into the Imperial-Uncle / belt-decree plot.
